task_completion.description: Open the 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image in ImageJ.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ interface showing an open image window titled 'Fluorescent Cells' or 'Fluorescent Cells (400K)' displaying a multichannel (red/green) cell image.

task_completion.description: Apply Gaussian Blur (sigma=2) and Otsu threshold to the Green channel (cell body).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the green channel isolated, a 'Gaussian Blur' dialog with sigma set to 2, and/or a thresholding dialog set to 'Otsu' resulting in a binary mask of the cell body.

task_completion.description: Generate a Euclidean Distance Map (EDM) from the thresholded cell body.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image window displaying a grayscale gradient map (Euclidean Distance Map), typically with darker/lighter regions radiating from the cell boundaries.

task_completion.description: Identify vesicles in the Red channel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the red channel isolated and thresholded, the 'Analyze Particles' dialog, or the ROI Manager populated with multiple selections corresponding to the vesicles.

task_completion.description: Measure the mean distance of the vesicles using the distance map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An ImageJ 'Results' window displaying measurements for the identified particles, specifically containing a 'Mean' column (which represents the mean intensity/distance from the EDM for each vesicle).

A 'Save As' dialog box saving the Results table to the path '~/ImageJ_Data/results/vesicle_distribution.csv' or a terminal/file explorer verifying the file's creation at '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/vesicle_distribution.csv'.

integrity.description: The agent used the ImageJ/Fiji graphical user interface to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of ImageJ menus, dialog boxes (like Gaussian Blur, Threshold, Analyze Particles), and native ImageJ image windows rather than just a terminal running a headless script.

integrity.description: The results table is generated by ImageJ's measurement tools, not manually fabricated.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Results' window is the standard ImageJ Java UI component, populated with multiple rows of floating-point data corresponding to the number of identified vesicles.

integrity.description: The analysis was performed on the actual 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The morphology of the cells in the binary masks and distance maps matches the distinct biological shapes of the built-in 'Fluorescent Cells' sample, not a generic or blank image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target image is the built-in 'Fluorescent Cells (400K)' accessed via File > Open Samples in ImageJ. It is a multichannel composite image with red and green channels. The final output must be saved to '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/vesicle_distribution.csv'. The required measurement column for the distance is simply named 'Mean' in ImageJ's Results Table (this represents the mean intensity of the Distance Map within each vesicle ROI, which equates to the mean distance from the boundary).
